title: ctr images ls cannot find the image that is builded by buildah
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
According this [article](https://wasmedge.org/book/en/kubernetes/demo/wasi.html), I have builded images.
```
sudo buildah images
REPOSITORY                    TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
localhost/wasm-wasi-example   latest   d230f65c92e0   24 hours ago   2.01 MB
```
The context of my Dockerfile is 
```
FROM scratch
ADD wasi_example_main.wasm /
CMD ["/wasi_example_main.wasm"]
```
By using the following command, I builded the image:
```
sudo buildah build --annotation "module.wasm.image/variant=compat" -t wasm-wasi-example .
```
However,when I want to use the command `ctr` to check whether the image is exit or not,
I didn't find it.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.download the example code 
```
git clone https://github.com/second-state/wasm-learning
cd wasm-learning/cli/add
```

2.build the wasm bytecode 
```
rustup target add wasm32-wasi
cargo build --target wasm32-wasi --release
```

3.change the mode of the wasm file
```
chmod +x target/wasm32-wasi/debug/add.wasm
```
4.create the dockerfile in the `target/wasm32-wasi/debug/` folder with the following content:
```
FROM scratch
ADD add.wasm /
CMD ["/add.wasm"]
```
5.[Build and install the latest buildah on Ubuntu](https://wasmedge.org/book/en/kubernetes/demo/wasi.html#build-and-install-the-latest-buildah-on-ubuntu)

6.Create  a container image with buildah
```
sudo buildah build --annotation "module.wasm.image/variant=compat" -t wasm-wasi-example .

```

**Describe the results you received:**

Of cousre, I actually find the image by the command`sudo buildah images`,
at the same time, i can create a container from this image,
`sudo buildah from localhost/wasm-wasi-example`
However, when I want to exec /add.wasm, an error  has occurred
```
exec container process `/add.wasm`: Exec format error
error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

I also try to use the command `ctr i ls ` to list all images,
but, i didn't find it.
I would like to know if `ctr` is supported to list the images that are builded by buildah and which tools we can use to create container except `buildah from ***`? 


**Describe the results you expected:**
```
ctr i ls
```
empty.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
apt list buildah
Listing... Done
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 amd64
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 arm64
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 armhf
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 s390x
```


**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.27.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.16.6
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.1
image Version:   5.21.2-dev
Git Commit:      2bd5083364d977d480a4fa0465fb9f035be50116
Built:           Wed Jul 13 10:00:27 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=20.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=focal
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 20.04 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="20.04 LTS (Focal Fossa)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 20.04 LTS"
VERSION_ID="20.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=focal
UBUNTU_CODENAME=focal
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux VM-0-17-ubuntu 5.4.0-109-generic #123-Ubuntu SMP Fri Apr 8 09:10:54 UTC 2022 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
Hi @JcJinChen , thanks for creating the issue.

I am not sure which command `ctr` you are referring to , could you share the full command ? But just curious why do you want to create a buildah container from a `wasm-compat` images.

I think `buildah images` should be enough to show if built image exists or not, then further you can use `podman` or any other container manager to run the image but underlying OCI runtime must support `wasm` so you need to make sure that `crun` with wasm support exists on your system and `crun --version` should be enough to tell you if your `crun` supports web assembly or not.
If you want to tryout running with `podman` you can try following steps: https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/main/docs/wasm-wasi-example.md#examples

> Hi @JcJinChen , thanks for creating the issue.
> 
> I am not sure which command `ctr` you are referring to , could you share the full command ? But just curious why do you want to create a buildah container from a `wasm-compat` images.
> 
> I think `buildah images` should be enough to show if built image exists or not, then further you can use `podman` or any other container manager to run the image but underlying OCI runtime must support `wasm` so you need to make sure that `crun` with wasm support exists on your system and `crun --version` should be enough to tell you if your `crun` supports web assembly or not.



> Hi @JcJinChen , thanks for creating the issue.
> 
> I am not sure which command `ctr` you are referring to , could you share the full command ? But just curious why do you want to create a buildah container from a `wasm-compat` images.
> 
> I think `buildah images` should be enough to show if built image exists or not, then further you can use `podman` or any other container manager to run the image but underlying OCI runtime must support `wasm` so you need to make sure that `crun` with wasm support exists on your system and `crun --version` should be enough to tell you if your `crun` supports web assembly or not.

Thanks for your reply. The commad `ctr` is cli tool of [containerd](https://github.com/containerd/containerd).
I have installed containerd on my system, the tool `ctr` can be used to list container, image and so on.
I am confusion that why  the `tool` ctr cannot be used to list image builded by `buildah`.
At the same time, I cannot use `tool` to run the image builded by `buildah`.

Actually, I also want to test these example in this [article](https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/main/docs/wasm-wasi-example.md), using podman to run the image.

Ah i see so `containerd` cannot see containers created by buildah since they use different storage underneath and they are different tools. You would need to push images build by buildah to local or remote repo and pull it again via `ctr` so they are loaded into containerd storage.

Since this issue is being discussed here: https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/966 I think this issue can be closed but please feel free to re-open.
If you use CRI-O instead of containerd, you will be able to see the images and still work with kubernetes.
